summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A rental company: profit P(x) = −2x² + 80x − 600 per unit rented. What is the break-even number of units?

−2x²+80x−600=0, x²−40x+300=0. x=(40±√(1600−1200))/2=(40±20)/2. x=10 or x=30.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Average rate of change of f(x) = x² from x = 1 to x = 3:

Average rate of change = [f(3)−f(1)]/(3−1) = (9−1)/2 = 4.

Example: A phone battery drains linearly from 100% to 0% in 10 hours. What percentage remains after 3.5 hours?

B(t) = 100 − 10t. B(3.5) = 100 − 35 = 65%.
